design_philosophy.core_loop: Deploy units on grid-based battlefields, position robots strategically, execute turn-based combat with weapon upgrades and spirit commands
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Tactical positioning and resource management through unit deployment on constrained maps; balancing offensive pressure against enemy waves with unit durability
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command an army of iconic giant robots from multiple anime franchises in crossover combat scenarios; customize and upgrade your mechanical forces
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Multiple Scenario system where player choice of mission order directly affects plot branching—narrative consequence baked into mission selection. First in series to feature weapon upgrades, giving persistent progression rewards. Blends narrative agency with tactical gameplay.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Weapon upgrade system allows players to scale unit power; Multiple Scenario branching may present varying difficulty paths depending on mission order selected

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon upgrades system—first in franchise to allow direct weapon enhancement between missions
progression.unlock_mechanism: Mission branching via Multiple Scenario system; player choice of scenario order unlocks different plot paths and presumably different unit acquisition opportunities
replayability_hooks: Multiple Scenario branching creates alternate narrative paths based on mission selection order
Weapon upgrade investments reward experimentation with different unit builds across playthroughs
Masō Kishin plot focus introduces lore depth for series-invested players

world_building_techniques: Crossover narrative integrating multiple anime franchises (Aura Battler Dunbine, GoShogun debuts in this title)
Multiple Scenario branching embeds narrative choice into gameplay—plot consequences tied to tactical decisions
Masō Kishin plot provides overarching lore framework across campaigns

faction_indicators: Multiple anime franchises represented as distinct unit rosters (Gundam series, Mazinger series, Getter Robo, new entries Aura Battler Dunbine and GoShogun)
Player allegiance to specific robot franchises influences unit roster and team composition
danger_signals: Mission branching creates FOMO—players may lock themselves into suboptimal plot paths via scenario selection order
environmental_storytelling: Backgrounds introduced in SRW series (per 3rd game notes) provide visual context for battles
Unit roster changes and availability tied to narrative progression through Multiple Scenario branching

sources_used: List of Super Robot Wars video games - Wikipedia
